name: Cause-Effect — Antibiotic Resistance Fishbone (7 branches)
input: Create a cause-effect fishbone diagram for antibiotic resistance: Overprescription, Agricultural Use, Incomplete Treatment Courses, Lack of New Antibiotics, Hospital-Acquired Infections, Poor Sanitation, Global Travel.
qualityCriteria: No placeholder or lorem ipsum content
napkinBenchmark: Fishbone diagram with 7 cause branches converging on antibiotic resistance effect.

data.title: Drivers of Antibiotic Resistance

items.label: Overprescription
items.description: Unnecessary antibiotic use for viral infections and mild conditions

items.label: Agricultural Use
items.description: Sub-therapeutic antibiotics in livestock feed for growth promotion

items.label: Incomplete Treatment
items.description: Patients stopping antibiotics early, promoting resistant survivor bacteria

items.label: R&D Pipeline Gap
items.description: Pharmaceutical companies underinvesting in new antibiotic development

items.label: Hospital-Acquired
items.description: Nosocomial infections spreading resistant strains in healthcare settings

items.label: Poor Sanitation
items.description: Inadequate water and sewage systems spreading resistant organisms

items.label: Global Travel
items.description: International movement disseminating resistance genes across borders

data.caption: Root causes of global antibiotic resistance crisis (Ishikawa fishbone analysis)
